title: December figures revealed for cargo boom at Prestwick Airport

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: The airport has seen huge growth in its cargo operations thanks to a number of vital contracts (Image: Prestwick Airport) The South Ayrshire airport announced in December it had returned a £3.5 million operating profit for the year to March 31, 2025, up £300,000 from the previous year. In the current financial year, it has already achieved its annual operating target in the first six months to September 2025.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: In the last 18 months, the airport has reinvested £10m in the airfield, ground services equipment and security to support its ambitious growth plans. The airport has successfully established itself as an alternative UK cargo gateway for the Asian market in the past year. In May, two Chinese flagship carriers opened bases at the airport bringing scheduled daily service to China, and there are now 16 weekly flights from the continent.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: The latest figures for December, released by the Civil Aviation Authority, continue to paint a bright picture for the airport's cargo operations. The airfield saw a total of 1,193 aircraft movements, with only 111 of these being military movements.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – Cargo boom at Prestwick Airport showing no signs of slowing down
